name: Legal Document AI
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Legal reasoning engine — contract clause analysis, risk identification, regulatory compliance assessment, legal summarization, obligation extraction, party identification, and jurisdictional analysis using GPT-4o for complex legal reasoning and GPT-4o-mini for classification and extraction tasks

dev.note: ~100K tokens/day, legal analysis prototyping with long documents

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput, batch contract processing at scale

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Legal knowledge retrieval — semantic search across contract libraries, case law precedents, regulatory databases, and internal legal opinions; hybrid search combining keyword matching for legal citations with vector similarity for conceptual legal queries

services.name: Azure Document Intelligence
services.purpose: Document parsing and extraction — OCR for scanned contracts, structured extraction of tables, signatures, dates, and parties from PDF/DOCX/TIFF legal documents; layout analysis for complex multi-column contracts and appendices

prod.note: ~10K pages/month, custom models for legal formats

services.name: Azure Blob Storage
services.purpose: Legal document repository — original contracts, processed documents, extracted clause libraries, OCR outputs, version history, and compliance audit archives with immutable storage for legal hold requirements

prod.note: ~500GB, production document archive with zone redundancy

enterprise.note: ~2TB, geo-redundant, WORM compliance, legal hold policies

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Legal analysis metadata — contract risk scores, extracted obligations and deadlines, clause classifications, review status tracking, reviewer assignments, audit trail of all AI-assisted legal decisions, and cross-contract relationship mapping

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: OpenAI API keys, storage account keys, search service keys, document processing credentials, and encryption keys for sensitive legal documents

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Pipeline observability — document processing throughput, AI analysis latency per document type, risk score distribution, clause extraction accuracy tracking, and legal review SLA compliance monitoring

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Document processing pipeline runtime — hosts the legal analysis microservices, document chunking workers, risk assessment engine, and API layer for the legal review interface

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for initial clause classification and entity extraction (party names, dates, amounts) — reserve GPT-4o for complex risk assessment and multi-clause reasoning that requires deep legal understanding

Implement document chunking with clause-aware boundaries — legal contracts have natural section breaks (recitals, definitions, covenants, representations) that preserve context better than fixed-size chunks, reducing re-read tokens by 40%

Cache frequently referenced legal templates and standard clause libraries in AI Search — most contracts in a domain (NDA, MSA, SaaS) share 60-70% common language, enabling reuse of prior analysis for standard clauses
Use Document Intelligence custom models trained on your specific contract formats — pre-trained layout extraction reduces GPT token consumption by providing structured input instead of raw OCR text
Process contracts in batch during off-peak hours for non-urgent reviews — queue documents and process overnight to benefit from lower contention and potential future reserved pricing
Implement incremental re-indexing for AI Search — when contract amendments arrive, re-index only changed sections rather than the full document, reducing search indexer compute by 80%
